title: The Devil Wears Nada

jokes.text: except his ex-wife
jokes.setup: Singing 'for he's a jolly good fellow, which nobody can deny'
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with 'except his ex-wife'
jokes.explanation: Clean subversion of the classic song with perfect economy. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: That's garbage, that's total garbage
jokes.setup: Character announces plan to learn Mandarin Chinese
jokes.punchline: Blunt dismissal of retirement plans
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat showing workplace dynamics. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Or that we made a few adjustments to the soda machine
jokes.setup: Listing things their hands-off supervisor didn't mind
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of stealing from soda machine
jokes.explanation: Nice euphemism for theft. Shows character through language choice.

jokes.text: Here's to us: the unsupervisables
jokes.setup: Toasting their group after losing their lenient supervisor
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'unsupervisables' perfectly captures their nature
jokes.explanation: Clever coinage that's both quotable and character-specific. Shows pride in their dysfunction.

jokes.text: much as what formerly was my kidney is now my heart
jokes.setup: Burns making an analogy about promotion from within
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to having organs rearranged
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line - casually mentioning impossible medical procedure. Very character-specific absurdism.

jokes.text: I dub thee king of the morons, also known as supervisor for sector 7-g
jokes.setup: Formal promotion ceremony
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest title followed by actual job description
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns insult disguised as honor. The juxtaposition of formal ceremony with brutal honesty works well.

jokes.text: The nitwits are running the goofhouse
jokes.setup: Reacting to Homer becoming supervisor
jokes.punchline: Colorful description of inmates running asylum
jokes.explanation: Fun wordplay on classic phrase. Economical and captures the situation well.

jokes.text: You know, I'd rather save my arm-lifting for tapping gauges or filling out employee evaluations
jokes.setup: Asked why he's not high-fiving in celebration
jokes.punchline: Overly serious justification for not participating in celebration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carl taking his new authority too seriously. The specificity sells it.

jokes.text: Yes, I have, in ways you're only beginning to suspect
jokes.setup: Denying that he hasn't changed since becoming supervisor
jokes.punchline: Ominous delivery suggesting dark transformation
jokes.explanation: Sets up Carl's power trip arc. Mildly ominous in a comedic way.

jokes.text: last year's cake and cookie sale raised $112 to fight childhood obesity
jokes.setup: Charity organization announcing fundraising success
jokes.punchline: Tiny amount raised for fighting obesity with cake and cookies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - fighting childhood obesity by selling cake and cookies, plus the pathetically small amount raised. Multi-layered joke.

jokes.text: We are a good-looking bunch
jokes.setup: Suggesting they do their own beefcake calendar
jokes.punchline: Self-assessment delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Simple self-delusion joke. Works through confidence in delivery but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Fine, I'll take off one glove
jokes.setup: Photographer asking for more sexiness
jokes.punchline: Marge's hilariously modest interpretation of 'sexier'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - completely misunderstanding what makes something sexy. Great character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: Well, it seems real to me
jokes.setup: Drinking wine described as 'andalusian'
jokes.punchline: Marge missing the geographical reference entirely
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Marge's naivety about wine culture. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Pop goes the Chupacabra
jokes.setup: Taking sexy photos with wine
jokes.punchline: Bizarre variation on 'Pop goes the weasel' with Mexican cryptid
jokes.explanation: Random but memorable phrase. The absurdity works even if the logic is unclear.

jokes.text: Well, not anymore. I changed the 'M' to a... to a 'P'
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his prank on the softball sheet
jokes.punchline: Proud explanation of childish name change
jokes.explanation: Very Homer-ish behavior but the joke itself is juvenile. Works for character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: When I grow up, I'm gonna drag you around and buy you clothes
jokes.setup: Being dragged around shopping
jokes.punchline: Baby threatening future revenge through shopping
jokes.explanation: Cute role-reversal joke. The specificity of the revenge (shopping and clothes) makes it work.

jokes.text: What's all the hubbub-aloo?
jokes.setup: Homer arriving to commotion
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'hullabaloo'
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer mispronunciation. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I certainly don't remember doing August
jokes.setup: Marge seeing herself in a calendar
jokes.punchline: Implication that August was particularly revealing/compromising
jokes.explanation: Good implication humor - suggests August was especially risqué without stating it directly.

jokes.text: Well, I got the next best thing
jokes.setup: Marge refusing to take clothes off
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy producing something as substitute
jokes.explanation: Setup for visual gag. Comic Book Guy's perverted nature is predictable but works in context.

jokes.text: Either you're my assistant or you find a job elsewhere. And in this economy, elsewhere ain't hiring
jokes.setup: Carl giving Homer ultimatum about job change
jokes.punchline: Economic reality check delivered bluntly
jokes.explanation: Topical humor about economic downturn. Shows Carl's power-trip character development effectively.

jokes.text: Either you're my assistant or you find a job elsewhere. And in this economy, elsewhere ain't hiring. - Fine, I guess I'm at your beck. - And call. No...!
jokes.setup: Homer reluctantly accepting, saying he's 'at your beck'
jokes.punchline: Carl completing the phrase, Homer's horrified reaction to implications
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on common phrase. Homer's growing realization of what he's agreed to works well.

jokes.text: the whole congregation has seen my scandinavian regions
jokes.setup: Marge embarrassed about calendar at church
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of private parts as 'Scandinavian regions'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant euphemism that's both geographically absurd and perfectly Marge-like in its awkward propriety.

jokes.text: Her shapely gams have finally persuaded me to switch from the julian calendar to the gregorian
jokes.setup: Continuing praise of Marge's calendar appearance
jokes.punchline: Absurdly academic response to attractive legs
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and erudite response. The calendar pun works on multiple levels - very Simpsons-style intellectual comedy.

jokes.text: I just heard your pants ain't hiring because your butt won't quit
jokes.setup: Another church member commenting on Marge
jokes.punchline: Mangled workplace metaphor for attractive posterior
jokes.explanation: Creative but confusing wordplay. The employment metaphor doesn't quite land cleanly.

jokes.text: His astrologist will show up at the same time as his astronomer
jokes.setup: Homer panicking about scheduling conflict
jokes.punchline: Carl apparently uses both astrologer and astronomer
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Carl's pretentiousness and Homer's confusion. The similar-sounding professions add to the comedy.

jokes.text: I'd like to watch Bart's mom washing her underwear
jokes.setup: History class about Washington crossing Delaware
jokes.punchline: Student's inappropriate sexual comment about Marge
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing consequences of calendar. Uncomfortable but appropriate for the storyline.

jokes.text: I'd give Bart's mom a general wow
jokes.setup: Teacher mentioning General Howe
jokes.punchline: Student's pun on the general's name
jokes.explanation: Clever pun on historical figure's name. The wordplay is solid even if the context is uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I'll have a quizzical expression for the rest of my life
jokes.setup: Student getting eyebrows stuck in raised position
jokes.punchline: Permanent facial expression as consequence
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy consequence. The specific facial expression 'quizzical' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: I'd like to get quizzical with Bart's mom
jokes.setup: Previous student's predicament with quizzical expression
jokes.punchline: Another student turning it into sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that turns innocent word into innuendo. Predictable and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Let me check my calendar
jokes.setup: Skinner needing to schedule appointment with Bart
jokes.punchline: Presumably checking Marge's sexy calendar
jokes.explanation: Good callback that works both as plot device and visual gag. Skinner's perverted side is well-established.

jokes.text: I'm suddenly feeling un-administrative feelings
jokes.setup: Skinner looking at Marge's calendar
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of sexual arousal
jokes.explanation: Good euphemism that fits Skinner's uptight personality. The bureaucratic language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Ocean view, obstructed
jokes.setup: Describing hotel room view
jokes.punchline: Real estate euphemism for bad view delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in real estate humor. The contradiction in terms is delivered perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: 'Lick... Eyes.' I'll try again. 'Spank... Hair'? What the...? 'whisper into ass'?
jokes.setup: Rolling dice for romantic activities
jokes.punchline: Increasingly nonsensical and impossible combinations
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from weird to impossible. The absurdity of the combinations and Homer's growing confusion work well.

jokes.text: This game sucks! Why don't you just roll me?
jokes.setup: Dice game producing impossible combinations
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated suggestion to roll him instead
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - his literal thinking and frustration come through clearly. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: That's right, we're going to fission week!
jokes.setup: Carl announcing nuclear expo in Paris
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'fashion week' with nuclear term
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works in context. Shows Carl's enthusiasm and ties to nuclear power plant setting.

jokes.text: Actually, those were Carl's shirts
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about ironing Homer's shirts
jokes.punchline: Homer revealing they were actually Carl's shirts
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that shows how degrading Homer's assistant job has become. Adds insult to injury effectively.

jokes.text: When I was your age, It was the future, 'cause I'm younger than you
jokes.setup: Carl making some point about age
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Nonsensical logic that sounds profound but isn't. Good character moment for pretentious Carl.

jokes.text: I don't have time for peeling. Squirt 'em in
jokes.setup: Homer offering Carl edamame
jokes.punchline: Carl's demanding way of eating, treating Homer like servant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carl's entitlement. The casual demand is both absurd and believable.

jokes.text: I'd put rocks into your pockets and walk you out to sea before that happened
jokes.setup: Boys mentioning Uncle Kevin might raise them
jokes.punchline: Ned's surprisingly dark threat about drowning them instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor from Ned, completely against character expectations. The specificity of the threat makes it both shocking and funny.

jokes.text: you're gonna see america's number one dishwashing crew! - I wash! - And I dry! Then I give 'em the squeak test and into the dishwasher they go
jokes.setup: Ned warning about their dishwashing efficiency
jokes.punchline: Elaborate hand-washing process before putting dishes in dishwasher
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders family humor - their obsessive efficiency defeats the purpose of having a dishwasher. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: still dead? - Yes, thank you for asking
jokes.setup: Carl asking about commissioner's wife
jokes.punchline: Polite exchange about wife's death
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor with perfect politeness. The casual treatment of death as ongoing condition is absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: closed on mondays. You are clearly a man of the world
jokes.setup: Carl claiming to love the Louvre
jokes.punchline: Only knowing basic tourist information, commissioner's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Carl's pretensions. The commissioner's dry response to basic tourist knowledge works well.

jokes.text: My assistant will do a sensual dance for your arousal
jokes.setup: French commissioner making increasingly forward advances
jokes.punchline: Carl offering Homer as sexual entertainment
jokes.explanation: Shows how far Carl's power trip has gone - literally pimping out Homer. Absurd but logical character escalation.

jokes.text: Le sexy rock and roll, the films of Claude Chabrol, Ooh la la, c'est dommage everybody eat fromage!
jokes.setup: French rock and roll song
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical French words forced into English song structure
jokes.explanation: Stereotypical French song parody. The forced rhyming and random French phrases work but aren't groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Rod watched a commercial for 'Grey's Anatomy,' Todd took a full day to tell me
jokes.setup: Explaining why the boys are grounded
jokes.punchline: Overprotective punishment for seeing commercial, delay in reporting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned humor - overprotective to absurd degree, and the boys' snitching dynamic. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: Gosh, what a hunk! I'm not thinking straight. Why did I drink that wine cooler last month?
jokes.setup: Ned attracted to someone
jokes.punchline: Blaming month-old wine cooler for impure thoughts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ned moment - his religious guilt making him blame ancient wine cooler for attraction. Perfect character humor.

jokes.text: She's the first lady of France, Carla Bruni!
jokes.setup: Homer threatening to expose Carl's affair
jokes.punchline: Revealing the woman is France's first lady
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that raises stakes. Topical reference that adds international scandal element.

jokes.text: he'll be all over you like Truffaut on Hitchcock
jokes.setup: Homer threatening Carl with presidential retaliation
jokes.punchline: Unlikely cultural reference from Homer about film directors
jokes.explanation: Great highbrow reference from lowbrow Homer. The specific film criticism knowledge is unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: Hello, you are getting cozy with Sarkozy
jokes.setup: Homer calling French president
jokes.punchline: Rhyming voicemail greeting
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun on the president's name. The rhyme works and fits the international comedy tone.

jokes.text: Ned, you got whipped cream in your mustache. Your perfectly trimmed mustache
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out whipped cream on Ned's mustache
jokes.punchline: Her sudden focus on his attractive facial hair
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Marge's growing attraction. The specific compliment about mustache trimming is nicely detailed.

jokes.text: My wife? And my worst friend? Could it be?
jokes.setup: Homer discovering apparent affair
jokes.punchline: Calling Ned his 'worst friend' instead of 'best friend'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of cliché. Homer's honesty about his relationship with Ned is both funny and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Want to kiss them for me?
jokes.setup: Homer about to roll dice
jokes.punchline: Marge's flirtatious suggestion about the dice
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier dice game with Marge now enthusiastic. Shows character arc completion.

tmdb_overview: Marge and a group called the "Charity Chicks" pose for a calendar in hopes of raising money for charity, but Marge becomes the talk of the town thanks to her racy poses. Meanwhile, Carl is chosen as the newest supervisor at the nuclear power plant, and hires Homer to be his personal assistant.
